title: 11 Herbal Essential Oils For Jaundice
intro_desc: Babies can get jaundice, a condition where their skin turns yellow due to too much bilirubin in their blood. This happens when their liver isn't working properly, and it can't remove the bilirubin. As a result, the bilirubin builds up and causes the yellow color. Jaundice is common in newborns, and it usually goes away on its own within a few weeks. However, in some cases, it can lead to serious health problems if left untreated. Babies with jaundice may seem okay, but they can be feeling unwell and uncomfortable. They may have trouble feeding, be irritable, and even have trouble sleeping. In severe cases, jaundice can cause brain damage or other complications, so it's essential to get it checked out.  When a baby has jaundice, their parents and healthcare providers may try various treatments. These can include light therapy, which exposes the baby to a special light that helps break down the bilirubin, and fluids to help the baby pass the bilirubin through their urine. However, some parents are looking for more natural ways to help their baby feel better. That's where herbal essential oils come in. These oils are derived from plants and can be used to help support the baby's liver function and reduce the bilirubin levels. They can also help to soothe and calm the baby, making them feel more comfortable and relaxed. When used properly and under the guidance of a healthcare provider, these oils may be a helpful addition to the baby's treatment plan.

remedies.remedy_desc: Silybum marianum essential oils contains flavonoids, sesquiterpenes, and silymarin, which are its active constituents that help with jaundice. The silymarin, in particular, has antioxidant properties that protect liver cells from damage and promote their regeneration. Jaundice occurs when bilirubin builds up in the blood, and Silybum marianum essential oils may help by supporting liver function and reducing bilirubin levels.  If you're considering using Silybum marianum essential oils for jaundice, consult with a healthcare professional first, especially if you're pregnant, breastfeeding, or taking any medications. To use it safely, dilute a few drops of the essential oil in a carrier oil and apply it topically to the liver area or take it internally in capsule form after consulting with a healthcare professional.  When used correctly, Silybum marianum essential oils may help alleviate jaundice symptoms, such as yellowing of the skin and eyes, by supporting liver health and reducing bilirubin levels. However, it's essential to note that Silybum marianum essential oils are not a substitute for medical treatment and should not be used without consulting a healthcare professional. If you experience severe symptoms, such as vomiting, dark urine, or pale stools, seek immediate medical attention.
remedies.image_alt: Silybum marianum herbal essential oils for jaundice

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale essential oils contains compounds like gingerol and shogaol, which have been found to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. These properties may help alleviate symptoms of jaundice by reducing liver inflammation and promoting bile flow. The constituents in Zingiber officinale essential oils, such as beta-sitosterol and zingiberene, may also help to stimulate digestion and reduce nausea and vomiting associated with jaundice. If you're considering using Zingiber officinale essential oils for jaundice, it's essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using it as a treatment. They may recommend using a small amount of the oil, typically 2-3 drops, mixed with a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil, and applied topically to the abdomen in a gentle circular motion. Alternatively, you can inhale the oil through steam inhalation or add a few drops to a warm bath for relaxation and potential relief from symptoms. When using Zingiber officinale essential oils for jaundice, it's likely that you may experience some relief from nausea and vomiting, as well as a reduction in abdominal pain and discomfort. However, it's crucial to note that jaundice can be a symptom of a more serious underlying condition, and you should continue to monitor your symptoms and seek medical attention if they worsen or persist.
remedies.image_alt: Zingiber officinale herbal essential oils for jaundice

remedies.remedy_desc: Curcuma longa essential oils contains a powerful compound called curcumin, which has potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. This helps in reducing the inflammation and oxidative stress that can cause jaundice. The essential oil is rich in other constituents like sesquiterpenes, terpenoids, and flavonoids, which also contribute to its therapeutic effects. When used in treating jaundice, Curcuma longa essential oils may help to reduce bilirubin levels and improve liver function.  To use Curcuma longa essential oils for jaundice, you can add a few drops to a warm bath or mix it with a carrier oil for a gentle massage. Start with small amounts and gradually increase as needed. It's essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using essential oils, especially if you're pregnant, breastfeeding, or taking medications. They can guide you on the right dosage and ensure it's safe for use.  Taking Curcuma longa essential oils may lead to a decrease in jaundice symptoms, such as yellowing of the skin and eyes, and an improvement in appetite and energy levels. Some people may experience relief from nausea and vomiting, while others might notice an improvement in their overall well-being. However, it's crucial to note that Curcuma longa essential oils should not be used as a replacement for medical treatment. If you're experiencing jaundice, it's essential to consult with a healthcare professional for proper diagnosis and treatment.
remedies.image_alt: Curcuma longa herbal essential oils for jaundice

remedies.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia essential oils contains linalool, linalyl acetate, and beta-caryophyllene, which are responsible for its potential benefits in reducing jaundice. Jaundice occurs when a buildup of bilirubin causes the skin and eyes to turn yellow, often due to liver dysfunction in newborns. The antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties of Lavandula angustifolia essential oils may help reduce bilirubin levels and alleviate liver congestion.  To use Lavandula angustifolia essential oils for jaundice, always consult a healthcare professional before applying it. For newborns, mix a few drops of the oil with a carrier oil and gently massage it onto the baby's skin, focusing on the liver area. For adults, inhale the oil through steam inhalation or apply it topically to the liver area after diluting it with a carrier oil.  When using Lavandula angustifolia essential oils for jaundice, you may notice a reduction in bilirubin levels, leading to a decrease in jaundice symptoms within a few days. As liver function improves, the skin and eyes may return to their normal color. However, it's essential to monitor the baby's condition closely and seek medical attention if the jaundice worsens or persists.
remedies.image_alt: Lavandula angustifolia herbal essential oils for jaundice

remedies.remedy_desc: Rosmarinus officinalis essential oils contains compounds like borneol, camphor, and pinene that may help alleviate jaundice in infants. These constituents possess anti-inflammatory properties, which can reduce swelling and discomfort in the liver and digestive system. Borneol, in particular, has been shown to have a hepatoprotective effect, helping to safeguard liver cells from damage. To try using Rosmarinus officinalis essential oils for jaundice, mix a few drops with a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil and gently massage it onto the baby's skin, focusing on the abdomen and chest areas. You can also add a few drops to a warm bath for a soothing and relaxing experience. However, it's crucial to consult with a healthcare professional or a certified aromatherapist before using essential oils on infants, as their skin is delicate and may react to certain ingredients. When using Rosmarinus officinalis essential oils for jaundice, you may notice a reduction in the baby's discomfort and distress. Their skin may appear lighter, and their digestive system may start to function more efficiently. In some cases, the jaundice may even resolve within a few days. However, it's essential to monitor the baby's condition closely and adjust the treatment plan as needed. Always prioritize the advice of a healthcare professional and ensure that the essential oils are used in a safe and responsible manner.
remedies.image_alt: Rosmarinus officinalis herbal essential oils for jaundice

remedies.remedy_desc: Mentha x piperita essential oils contains properties and constituents such as menthol, menthone, and limonene, which have antispasmodic, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant effects. These properties may help alleviate jaundice by reducing inflammation in the liver and promoting bile flow, thus potentially lowering bilirubin levels.  To use Mentha x piperita essential oils for jaundice, mix a few drops with a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil and apply it to the abdomen or liver area. You can also inhale the oil by adding a few drops to a diffuser. However, it's essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using essential oils, especially when treating a medical condition like jaundice. If you follow this advice, the likely outcomes may include a reduction in jaundice symptoms, such as yellowing of the skin and eyes, and an improvement in liver function. However, it's crucial to note that jaundice can be a symptom of a more serious underlying condition, and this treatment should not be used as a replacement for medical care. Monitor your symptoms and consult with a healthcare professional if you experience any worsening of symptoms or concerns.
remedies.image_alt: Mentha x piperita herbal essential oils for jaundice

remedies.remedy_desc: Artemisia absinthium essential oils contains compounds like thujone, camphor, and borneol, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. These properties help reduce bilirubin levels in the blood, a key factor in jaundice. The high concentration of thujone, a known hepatoprotective agent, protects liver cells from damage, promoting the removal of excess bilirubin and helping to alleviate jaundice symptoms.  To use Artemisia absinthium essential oils for jaundice, mix a few drops with a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil and apply it topically to the liver area. You can also inhale the oil through steam inhalation or add it to a warm bath for relaxation and stress relief, which can help alleviate jaundice symptoms. However, it is essential to note that while essential oils can provide relief, they should not replace medical treatment. If you are experiencing jaundice, consult a healthcare professional before using Artemisia absinthium essential oils. Taking action with Artemisia absinthium essential oils may lead to a reduction in bilirubin levels and alleviation of jaundice symptoms, such as pale skin, dark urine, and yellowing of the eyes. However, it is crucial to be patient and consistent, as the effects may take time to manifest. Also, be aware that individual results may vary, and some people may not respond to the treatment. It is also essential to follow proper dilution and usage guidelines to avoid adverse reactions.
remedies.image_alt: Artemisia absinthium herbal essential oils for jaundice

remedies.remedy_desc: Melissa officinalis essential oils contains properties and constituents that may help alleviate jaundice symptoms in newborns. The oil's high antioxidant content, particularly linalool and linalyl acetate, has been shown to promote liver function and reduce oxidative stress, which can contribute to jaundice. Melissa officinalis essential oil also has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help soothe and calm the digestive system, further reducing the severity of jaundice.  If you're considering using Melissa officinalis essential oils to help with jaundice, it's essential to consult with a healthcare professional before doing so. They may recommend a gentle and diluted application of the oil to the skin, or suggest inhaling the oil in a controlled environment. It's crucial to follow these guidelines to ensure safe use and optimal effectiveness.  Taking action with Melissa officinalis essential oils may lead to a reduction in jaundice symptoms, such as a decrease in bilirubin levels and an improvement in overall liver function. However, it's also possible that jaundice symptoms may persist or worsen if the underlying cause is not addressed. Regular monitoring by a healthcare professional is necessary to assess the effectiveness of the treatment and make any necessary adjustments.
remedies.image_alt: Melissa officinalis herbal essential oils for jaundice

remedies.remedy_desc: Achillea millefolium essential oils contains compounds that may help alleviate jaundice symptoms in infants. The oil's active constituents, including sesquiterpene lactones and flavonoids, have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. These properties may help reduce liver congestion and promote bile flow, which can be beneficial for treating jaundice.  If you're considering using Achillea millefolium essential oils for jaundice, it's essential to consult with a healthcare professional before applying it to your baby. A small, diluted amount of the oil may be added to a warm bath or applied topically to the baby's skin, after being diluted with a carrier oil. However, it's crucial to note that the oil should not be ingested or used as a replacement for medical treatment. Taking action with Achillea millefolium essential oils may lead to reduced jaundice symptoms, such as a decrease in skin yellowing and improved overall health. However, it's also possible that no improvement may be observed, and the oil may cause skin irritation in some individuals. Regular monitoring of your baby's condition and consulting with a healthcare professional is necessary to ensure the safety and efficacy of this treatment.
remedies.image_alt: Achillea millefolium herbal essential oils for jaundice

remedies.remedy_desc: Cymbopogon citratus essential oils contains properties and constituents that can help alleviate jaundice symptoms in infants. The oil contains compounds like geraniol, limonene, and beta-pinene, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. These properties can help reduce liver inflammation and promote the breakdown of bilirubin, a pigment that causes jaundice.  When using Cymbopogon citratus essential oils for jaundice, it's essential to consult a healthcare professional before application. A small amount of the oil (about 1-2 drops per 100ml of carrier oil) can be applied topically to the infant's skin, usually on the abdomen or back, after diluting it with a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil.  However, it's crucial to note that Cymbopogon citratus essential oils should not replace medical treatment for jaundice. If your infant's jaundice persists or worsens, seek immediate medical attention.  Using Cymbopogon citratus essential oils for jaundice may lead to a reduction in bilirubin levels, which can result in improved skin color and a decrease in symptoms like lethargy and poor appetite.
remedies.image_alt: Cymbopogon citratus herbal essential oils for jaundice

remedies.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus globulus essential oils contains compounds like cineole, eucalyptol, and limonene, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. These properties may help with jaundice by reducing liver inflammation and promoting the body's natural detoxification processes. Cineole, in particular, has been shown to have hepatoprotective effects, meaning it may help protect the liver from damage. When considering using Eucalyptus globulus essential oils for jaundice, it's essential to consult with a healthcare professional before taking any action. They may recommend a diluted form of the essential oil, typically 1-2% dilution with a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil, for external use, such as applying it to the abdomen or liver area. However, if advised by a healthcare professional, you can ingest it after dilution. It is crucial to follow their guidance. If you decide to proceed under the guidance of a healthcare professional, you may experience reduced liver inflammation and improved liver function. This may result in a decrease in jaundice symptoms, such as yellowing of the skin and eyes. You may also notice an improvement in your overall well-being, including a reduction in fatigue and a return to normal bowel movements. However, it's essential to remember that each person's response may vary, and results should not be expected without medical supervision.
remedies.image_alt: Eucalyptus globulus herbal essential oils for jaundice

faq_prevent: Drinking herbal tea may help reduce the risk of jaundice in newborns. Some herbal teas, like dandelion and milk thistle, have been shown to support liver health. These teas may aid the liver in removing bilirubin, the pigment that causes jaundice. However, more research is needed to confirm their effectiveness.
faq_safe: Herbal teas for jaundice are not a substitute for medical treatment. While some herbs like milk thistle may help, consuming them daily is not a safe solution. The liver needs specific medical care to recover from jaundice. Herbal teas might interact with medications or worsen the condition if not used properly.
faq_timeline: Herbal teas can help with jaundice, but it's hard to say exactly how long it takes to see results. Some people notice improvements in a few days, while others may take a week or more to feel better. It really depends on the severity of the jaundice and the individual's response to the herbal teas.
faq_time: Drinking herbal tea for jaundice is best in the morning. This helps your body process the tea's active ingredients throughout the day, potentially reducing jaundice symptoms. Morning also gives you a chance to start the day feeling refreshed and more alert.
faq_interaction: Some herbal teas may interact with medications for jaundice. For example, St. John's Wort can make certain medications less effective. Licorice root can also increase the risk of liver damage when taken with certain medications. These interactions can be serious, so it's essential to be aware of the potential risks when combining herbal teas with prescription medications.
faq_pregnant: It's not recommended to drink herbal teas for jaundice while pregnant. Some herbal teas can be too strong and might affect the baby's health. Jaundice is a condition that needs medical attention, and herbal teas might not be enough to treat it. It's best to wait for a medical check-up and follow the doctor's advice.
faq_breastfeeding: Herbal teas for jaundice while breastfeeding aren't recommended. Some herbs can pass into breast milk and affect the baby. For example, dandelion root tea can lower breast milk production and sage tea can slow down digestion in the baby. It's best to choose teas that are safe for breastfeeding mothers and their babies.
title_2: Baby Jaundice and Herbal Essential Oils: 11 Remedies to Help
